page_summary: A page displaying a column of prime-factorization fractions flanked by structural formulas of the four DNA nucleotide bases (adenine, guanine, cytosine, thymine).

decorations.description: Structural formula of adenine (6-aminopurine): a fused bicyclic purine ring system with an NH2 substituent and an N-H in the five-membered imidazole ring, with HC and CH labels on ring carbons.
Structural formula of guanine (2-amino-6-oxopurine): a fused bicyclic purine ring system with an NH2 group, two ring C=O groups, ring NH, and CH/HC labels.
Structural formula of cytosine (4-amino-2-oxopyrimidine): a single six-membered pyrimidine ring with an NH2 group, a C=O group, an N-H, and CH/HC labels.
Structural formula of thymine (5-methyl-2,4-dioxopyrimidine): a single six-membered pyrimidine ring with a CH3 group, two C=O groups, an N-H, and CH/HC labels.

uncertain_or_unreadable: It is unclear what the column of fractions represents mathematically (e.g., factorizations of binomial coefficients, factorials, or a single large number); the pattern of common prime factors (29, 101, 239, 281, 4649, 909091, 121499449) in many denominators suggests a shared underlying value, but the source/caption is not visible on the page.
